answer:
**Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation*, Volume II, Chapter 2 ("Des limites mises par la nature au perfectionnement des facultés humaines"), argues that European colonization in America, southern Asia, islands of the great Ocean, and southern and western Africa has created "a kind of aristocracy of which we had no idea, **the aristocracy of species**".**[^1]

This new social ordering, arising from interactions among "diverse classes of the population" now spanning different "species of men," is predicted to "considerably influence the existence of the American republics."[^1]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/02-des-limites-mises-par-la-nature-au-perfectionnemen/translation/#p-44e2ce).
